target.name: Cerebras
target.founder: Andrew Feldman, Gary Lauterbach, Michael James, Sean Lie, Jean-Philippe Fricker

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $5.0B - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Rebellions
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Pre-IPO), post-money $2.34B

data.narrative:
Cerebras is a pre-IPO semiconductor company founded by Andrew Feldman and others, currently valued at $23B after raising $1.1B+. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public semiconductor firms (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private players in chips and frontier compute infrastructure.

Peer-median valuation represents the primary applicable method. Four same-sector comparables with similar valuation tier—PsiQuantum ($7.0B, private), Etched ($5.0B, Series A), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), and Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B, public)—yield a median of $5.0B and a range of $5.0B to $7.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Cerebras does not publicly disclose revenue, precluding meaningful price-to-sales analysis against the three public semiconductor tickers (sector P/S median: 22.3x).

Triangulation on a single method implies a range of $4.2B to $5.8B with a median of $5.0B. The current $23B valuation substantially exceeds this peer-anchored range, creating a material gap. Key uncertainties include whether Cerebras commands a premium reflective of proprietary architecture or market position not captured in peer multiples, and the absence of revenue disclosure limiting revenue-based cross-checks. A more granular assessment would require access to financial metrics or customer concentration data.
